question: Compare and contrast the hierarchical data model with the network data model. Identify one key advantage and one key limitation of each.
expectedPoints: Hierarchical Model: Advantage - Simple and fast for data with tree-like structures (e.g., company organizational chart). Limitation - Inflexible; cannot easily represent complex many-to-many relationships.

Network Model: Advantage - More flexible than hierarchical; can represent complex many-to-many relationships using owner-member sets. Limitation - Highly complex; the database structure becomes very difficult to design and manage.
Core Difference: Hierarchical is a tree structure where a child has only one parent, while Network is a graph structure allowing a child to have multiple parents.

question: Explain the fundamental components of the Entity-Relationship (ER) model. Use a simple example (e.g., Students and Courses) to illustrate these components.
expectedPoints: Attributes: Properties describing an entity (e.g., Student ID, Course Name, Student Name).
Relationships: Associations between entities (e.g., 'Enrolls_in' connecting Student and Course).
Cardinality: Defines the numerical relationship between entities (e.g., One-to-Many: one student can enroll in many courses, and one course can have many students).

question: Describe the primary objectives and components of the Three-Schema Architecture. Explain how this architecture provides logical and physical data independence.
expectedPoints: Objective: To separate the user applications from the physical database, enabling data independence and abstracting different views of data.
Internal Schema: Describes the physical storage structure and access paths (e.g., indexes).
Conceptual Schema: Describes the logical structure of the entire database for the community of users (entities, data types, relationships, constraints).
External Schema: Describes the view of data for specific user groups, hiding irrelevant parts of the conceptual schema.
Logical Independence: The capacity to change the conceptual schema without affecting external schemas or application programs.
Physical Independence: The capacity to change the internal schema without changing the conceptual schema.
